raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California health professionals are committed to making firearm violence prevention part of their practice.",
    "Medical and mental health providers are uniquely positioned to both respond to and prevent firearm-related harm.",
    "Many medical providers cite lack of knowledge regarding when and how to discuss firearms with their patients as a principal barrier to action.",
    "Major medical associations have issued statements calling for a public health approach to reducing firearm-related injury and death.",
    "Expanded education and training are needed to improve clinician familiarity with the benefits and risks of firearm ownership, safety practices, and communication with patients about firearm violence.",
    "Medical schools and residency programs should incorporate firearm violence prevention into their academic curricula.",
    "California established the first-in-the-nation center devoted to firearms research in 2016.",
    "The estimated cost of hospital and emergency department care for firearm-related injuries in California was $112 million in 2010."
  ]
}
```
